content.full_text:
TO: File

DATE: November 1, 1982
File No. T-82-180

FROM: Fair Political Practices Commission
Janis Shank McLean

RE: Contingency Fee

A contingency agreement between a lawyer and client should be considered to be a "promise to pay" $250 or more. The reasons are:

1. 1/3 (typical contingency fee amount) of almost any judgment will amount to $250 or more;

2. Attorneys usually only take cases in which they are reasonably certain of collecting a fee.

A circumstance that may change this analysis is if the attorney is representing a family member, or friend, and does not care about the money.

JSM:plh
